title: Manufacturing Process (Simplified Input)
description: A user-friendly form to describe a manufacturing process step. Provide a name, optional description, and lists of input and output material IRIs. This input will be automatically converted to a standardised RDF representation (PMDCo OO-LD).

process_name.title: Process Name
process_name.description: A human-readable name for this process step. Example: 'Sintering and Annealing batch 1', 'Hydrogen Decrepitation'.

process_id.title: Process ID
process_id.description: Optional. Identifier (IRI slug) for the process node in the RDF graph. If omitted, derived automatically from process_name (e.g. 'Sintering and Annealing' → 'process-sintering-and-annealing').

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional free-text description of what this process does.
examples: Green compacts are heated below the melting point, causing particles to fuse. Annealing follows to relieve stresses.

inputs.title: Input Material IRIs
inputs.description: IRIs of materials consumed or used by this process. Reference existing knowledge-graph nodes.

outputs.title: Output Material IRIs
outputs.description: IRIs of materials produced by this process. Reference existing knowledge-graph nodes.

preceded_by.title: Preceded By (Process IRIs)
preceded_by.description: IRIs of processes that directly precede this one in the process chain.

conditions.title: Process Conditions / Parameters
conditions.description: Quantitative conditions under which this process is carried out (e.g. temperature, pressure, energy consumption).

name.title: Parameter Name
name.description: Name of the process condition (e.g. 'Sintering Temperature', 'Applied Pressure').

examples.description: Green compacts are heated below the melting point, causing particles to fuse together (sintering). Annealing follows to relieve internal stresses and optimise microstructure.
